title: oxytropis campestris var. viscida
intro: Oxytropis campestris var. viscida, also known as sticky globular milkvetch, is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family Fabaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that provide various health benefits, such as antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help to boost the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and alleviate symptoms of various diseases. Herbal preparations of Oxytropis campestris var. viscida can be made by infusing the dried roots or leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant in the form of tinctures, decoctions, or infusions.
